prompt: Steel frame fire protection method selection (intumescent vs board) impacts:

options.text: Cost, programme, thickness, and maintainability of steel sections

explanation: Fire protection systems affect cost, programme, and spatial coordination.

distractorExplanations.a: Fire protection affects build-up, cost, and programme.
distractorExplanations.d: Fire protection is mandatory cost and programme item.

prompt: Client funded retention on PFI differs from standard JCT because:

options.text: Identical to JCT without reading contract
Payment mechanisms follow project agreement output specs and lifecycle service deductions
QS not involved in payment certification

explanation: PFI finance control follows project agreement, not standard JCT alone.

distractorExplanations.a: PFI has rigorous payment and performance reporting.
distractorExplanations.b: PFI payment structures differ from JCT building contracts.
distractorExplanations.d: QS/certifier roles exist per project agreement.

prompt: Value engineering on MEP plant room spatial constraints should consider:

options.text: Only smallest plant regardless of maintenance
Exclude builders work in walls for penetrations
Equipment access, replacement routes, duct/pipe coordination, and builder's work allowances

explanation: Plant room VE balances capital, spatial, and maintainability constraints.

distractorExplanations.a: Maintainability affects lifecycle cost and operations.
distractorExplanations.b: Builders work is real cost at interfaces.
distractorExplanations.c: Plant selection must meet performance and maintenance needs.

prompt: Payment application underpayment dispute — subcontractor cash remedy may include:

options.text: Adjudication, suspension notice per statute, or negotiated settlement per sub terms
Remove works without notice always lawful

explanation: Payment disputes use contractual, adjudication, and statutory remedies.

prompt: Building Regulations Part L compliance strategy affects QS cost plan via:

options.text: No cost impact on any element
Enhanced envelope, HVAC efficiency, renewables, and testing/commissioning allowances
Eliminates need for air tightness testing

explanation: Regulatory energy targets materially affect envelope and services costs.

distractorExplanations.a: Part L drives specification and systems cost.
distractorExplanations.c: Part L focuses on energy performance of fabric and systems.
distractorExplanations.d: Testing and commissioning are Part L related costs.

prompt: Earned value metric CPI < 1 on contractor project indicates:

options.text: Cost efficiency below plan — spending more per unit of work than baseline

explanation: CPI below 1 means actual cost exceeds earned value baseline.

distractorExplanations.a: CPI addresses cost efficiency, not schedule directly.
distractorExplanations.b: CPI below 1 signals cost overrun efficiency.
distractorExplanations.d: CPI is cost performance index, not cash timing.

prompt: Post-tensioned slab construction technology advantage is:

options.text: Cheaper than all alternatives without analysis
Longer spans, thinner slabs, and reduced reinforcement for open plan layouts

explanation: PT slabs enable longer spans with structural and cost implications.

distractorExplanations.a: PT slabs require specialist design and installation.
distractorExplanations.b: PT is common in commercial/car park construction.
distractorExplanations.c: Cost depends on spans, logistics, and specialist trade.

prompt: Monthly valuation meeting agenda for contractor QS should cover:

options.text: Progress measure, variations, claims status, payment forecast, and supply chain applications

explanation: Valuation meetings coordinate progress, variations, and payment forecasting.

distractorExplanations.b: Valuation meetings focus on commercial progress.
distractorExplanations.c: Meeting addresses project valuation and payment.
distractorExplanations.d: Current project valuation is the priority.

prompt: RICS APC Level 3 project finance evidence requires showing:

options.text: Leading cost reporting, cash flow forecasting, and final account resolution on real project

explanation: Level 3 project finance is demonstrated through live reporting and account closure.

distractorExplanations.a: Level 3 needs applied leadership with outcomes.
distractorExplanations.c: Level 3 includes advice and reporting to stakeholders.
distractorExplanations.d: Final account is core project finance competency.

prompt: Facade access strategy (BMU vs cradle) lifecycle cost includes:

options.text: No cost because landlord maintains always
Capital install, maintenance, replacement, and statutory inspection regime

explanation: Facade access systems carry capital and recurring compliance/maintenance costs.

distractorExplanations.a: BMU/cradle systems have ongoing maintenance and inspection costs.
distractorExplanations.b: Responsibility depends on lease/contract — costs still exist.
distractorExplanations.d: Access systems are construction and FM cost items.

prompt: Cost report variance analysis 'committed vs uncommitted' helps employer:

options.text: Understand how much scope is fixed by orders vs still exposed to market movement

explanation: Committed/uncommitted analysis shows forecast certainty and exposure.

distractorExplanations.b: Uncommitted scope still needs forecast allowance.

prompt: SFS (light steel framing) infill construction technology is suited to:

options.text: Rapid envelope closure in panelised residential/commercial infill with disciplined interfaces

explanation: SFS enables fast infill construction with interface coordination requirements.

distractorExplanations.b: SFS is infill framing, not heavy foundation systems.
distractorExplanations.c: SFS requires structural design within limits.
distractorExplanations.d: SFS external walls need full weathertight build-up.

prompt: Project cash flow sensitivity to 4-week programme delay on high-prelims project:

options.text: Extended preliminaries, labour/plant standing time, and potential LD exposure

explanation: Programme delay drives preliminaries extension and LD risk.

distractorExplanations.c: Delay generally increases cost and risk.
distractorExplanations.d: LDs typically apply contractor-to-employer for delay.

prompt: Final account negotiation stalemate on £400k variation. Next step per JCT culture?

options.text: Document positions, consider adjudication, mediation, or senior commercial settlement

explanation: Final account disputes follow negotiation, ADR, and adjudication routes.

distractorExplanations.a: Parties should pursue contractual dispute resolution.
distractorExplanations.d: Records are essential for dispute resolution.

prompt: Photovoltaic array on flat roof — QS should allow for:

options.text: Panels only with no electrical scope
Structural load check, ballasted/penetrating mounts, inverter, cabling, and DNO connection

explanation: PV costing includes structural, electrical, and connection components.

distractorExplanations.a: PV includes structural, electrical, and grid connection costs.
distractorExplanations.b: Maintenance and safety access may be required.

prompt: Cost plan vs tender vs actual 'triangle' reporting shows:

options.text: Budget trajectory from planning through procurement to forecast final outturn

explanation: Cost plan-tender-actual triangle tracks cost performance through project stages.

distractorExplanations.b: Triangle tracks cost performance through project life.
distractorExplanations.c: Triangle is project cost benchmarking tool.

prompt: Waterproof concrete basement tanking vs membrane system — technology choice affects:

options.text: Risk profile, joint detailing, repairability, and warranty approach
Nothing — identical cost and risk always

explanation: Basement waterproofing system choice affects risk, detail, and cost profile.

distractorExplanations.a: Waterproofing strategy affects basement performance risk.
distractorExplanations.c: Systems have different risk and detailing implications.
distractorExplanations.d: Basement waterproofing is substructure/envelope interface issue.

prompt: Interim payment certificate delay of 35 days beyond due date triggers:

options.text: Statutory interest and potential suspension rights per HGCRA on qualifying contracts
Contractor must continue funding indefinitely without remedy

explanation: Late payment under construction act contracts carries interest and remedy rights.

distractorExplanations.a: Remedies follow notice and statutory thresholds.
distractorExplanations.b: Late payment statutes provide interest remedies.

prompt: Acoustic floor build-up in residential conversion requires costing:

options.text: Insulation, resilient bars, floating deck, flanking paths, and testing allowance

explanation: Acoustic compliance requires specified build-ups and testing allowances.

distractorExplanations.a: Residential acoustic floors need compliant build-up.
distractorExplanations.b: Flanking transmission is major acoustic cost/risk.
distractorExplanations.c: Part E drives acoustic performance requirements.

prompt: Supply chain insolvency during project — QS commercial response includes:

options.text: Assess exposure, retender packages, value acceleration cost, and update forecast/cash flow

explanation: Supply chain failure triggers retender, cost, and programme reassessment.

distractorExplanations.d: Retender in distress market may cost more and delay programme.

prompt: Modular bathroom pods technology benefit for programme is:

options.text: Parallel manufacture while site works proceed, reducing critical path duration
Unlimited design changes on site without cost

explanation: Pods compress programme via offsite manufacture parallel to site works.

distractorExplanations.a: Pods still require site interfaces and coordination.
distractorExplanations.c: Wet areas still require testing and commissioning.
distractorExplanations.d: Late changes to pods carry significant cost.

prompt: Employer wants KPI dashboard: CPI, committed cost, FFC, cash flow. QS provides:

options.text: Four conflicting spreadsheets with different FFC definitions
Integrated monthly report with definitions consistent across project stakeholders

explanation: Project finance dashboards need consistent metric definitions and monthly refresh.

distractorExplanations.d: Dashboards show dynamic forecast, not static tender.

prompt: Thermal bridging at balcony connections should be costed with:

options.text: Standard balcony without thermal break always
Structural thermal break details, insulation continuity, and condensation risk mitigation

explanation: Balcony thermal bridges require specialist detailing with cost implications.

distractorExplanations.a: Part L and performance specs may require thermal breaks.
distractorExplanations.b: Balcony connections are major thermal bridge risk.

prompt: RICS QS reviewing contractor payment application should verify:

options.text: Application matches site progress, contract rates, variations authorised, and retention deducted

explanation: Payment certification verifies progress, authority, and contractual deductions.

distractorExplanations.b: Certification requires progress and contractual verification.

prompt: Green roof intensive vs extensive technology cost difference driven by:

options.text: Growing medium depth, plant species, irrigation, structural loading, and maintenance

explanation: Green roof type affects structure, irrigation, and maintenance cost.

distractorExplanations.a: Intensive systems are heavier and maintenance-intensive.
distractorExplanations.c: Intensive green roofs cost more to build and maintain.
distractorExplanations.d: Loading drives structural design and cost.
